content.body:
Implement Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) as a first-class deployment target within StyxCD. The workflow should support provisioning, deployment, validation, rollback, and future promotion strategies while remaining consistent with the existing execution-plan architecture.

Acceptance Criteria:

- GKE is supported as a first-class StyxCD deployment target.
- Applications can be deployed to GKE through a StyxCD workflow.
- Authentication and credential management strategy is defined.
- Deployment validation is supported.
- Rollback capability is supported.
- Workflow DSL supports GKE-specific configuration.
- End-to-end deployment can be demonstrated using StyxCD.
content.title: Epic: GKE Workflow

content.body:
Description:

Replace the current hello-world GKE stage with a real implementation that executes gcloud and kubectl commands. The stage should deploy an application to a target GKE cluster using workflow configuration provided by the orchestrator.

Acceptance Criteria:

- GKE stage executes real deployment commands.
- Deployment parameters are supplied through the execution plan.
- Stage reports success and failure through existing callback mechanisms.
- Deployment can be demonstrated end-to-end.
content.title: Implement GKE deployment stage

content.body:
Description:

Determine how StyxCD authenticates to GKE clusters. Evaluate service accounts, workload identity, and credential management approaches.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Authentication approach documented.
- Credentials are not stored in workflow definitions.
- Solution supports automated deployments.
- Security considerations documented.
content.title: Define GKE Authentication Strategy

content.body:
Deploy a sample application to GKE using StyxCD to validate the complete workflow from YAML submission through deployment execution.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Application successfully deployed.
- Deployment initiated through StyxCD workflow.
- Application reachable after deployment.
- Workflow execution recorded by orchestrator.
content.title: Deploy Sample Application to GKE

content.body:
Design and implement rollback capabilities for GKE deployments.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Rollback strategy documented.
- Rollback can be triggered through workflow configuration.
- Previous deployment version can be restored.
- Rollback status reported to orchestrator.
content.title: Add GKE Rollback Support

content.body:
Implement deployment validation after application rollout.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Validation stage executes after deployment.
- Health checks are configurable.
- Validation results reported to orchestrator.
- Failed validation marks deployment unsuccessful.
content.title: Add GKE Deployment Validation

content.body:
Define how GKE deployments are represented within the StyxCD workflow DSL.

Acceptance Criteria:

- GKE workflow model documented.
- Required and optional fields identified.
- Example workflow provided.
- Model aligns with future multi-cloud support.
content.title: Define GKE Deployment Model

content.body:
Provide a repeatable sandbox environment for developing and testing GKE workflows.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Sandbox deployment process documented.
- Test workflow executes successfully.
- Environment can be recreated consistently.
- Suitable for future automated testing.
content.title: Implement GKE Sandbox Environment Support

content.body:
Implement the security foundation for StyxCD. Define how users, services, execution engines, and external systems authenticate and authorize actions within the platform. The design should support both a single-user deployment model and future multi-user enterprise deployments.

Acceptance Criteria
- Authentication strategy is defined.
- Service-to-service authentication is implemented.
- Sensitive credentials are managed securely.
- Authorization model is documented.
- Jenkins and future execution engines can be trusted and verified.
- Security architecture supports future enterprise requirements.
- Security design is documented.
content.title: Epic: Platform Security

content.body:
Determine how users authenticate to the StyxCD control plane. Evaluate API keys, OAuth2, OIDC, and other approaches that support both personal and enterprise deployments.

Acceptance Criteria
- Authentication options evaluated.
- Recommended approach documented.
- Enterprise requirements considered.
- User experience considerations documented.
content.title: Issue: Evaluate Control Plane Authentication Strategy

items.title: Issue: Evaluate Control Plane Authentication Strategy

content.body:
Implement authentication for StyxCD APIs and UI access based on the selected authentication strategy. The solution should support both personal deployments and future enterprise deployments.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unauthenticated requests are rejected.
- Authenticated users can access authorized resources.
- Authentication integrates with the UI.
- Authentication is covered by automated tests.
- Authentication approach is documented.

content.body:
Define how StyxCD services authenticate with one another, including orchestrator, Jenkins, future execution engines, agents, and external integrations.

content.body:
Implement reusable workflow templates and an Effective YAML capability. Templates should allow platform teams to define standard workflow structures while application teams provide only the values that vary. StyxCD should render and display the fully resolved workflow before execution.

Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow templates are supported.
- Variable substitution is supported.
- Template inheritance/composition is supported.
- Effective YAML can be generated before execution.
- Effective YAML can be viewed in the UI.
- Users can validate resolved workflows prior to execution.
- Template design supports future multi-cloud workflows.
content.title: Epic: Workflow Templates & Effective YAML

content.body:
Define the template architecture for StyxCD. Determine how templates, values, defaults, and overrides interact within workflow definitions.

- Override behavior defined.

content.body:
Implement the backend logic that resolves templates and values into a final workflow definition suitable for planning and execution.

- Templates can be resolved into executable workflows.

content.body:
Generate a fully resolved workflow definition after template processing and before execution. The generated YAML should represent the exact workflow that will be executed.

content.body:
## Goal

Build the first production-quality version of **Kharon**, the AI knowledge assistant for StyxCD.

Kharon is an independent Python service responsible for retrieval, reasoning,
knowledge ingestion, and AI interactions. StyxCD remains the deterministic
deployment platform while Kharon provides AI-assisted capabilities.

**Guiding Principle**

> StyxCD authorizes.  
> Kharon advises.

---

# Success Criteria

- Standalone Python service
- Chat endpoint operational
- RAG implemented using StyxCD documentation
- PostgreSQL + pgvector knowledge store
- LangChain and LangGraph integrated
- Able to answer StyxCD-specific questions using retrieved documentation

---
content.title: Epic: Kharon V1 - AI Knowledge Assistant

items.title: Epic: Kharon V1 - AI Knowledge Assistant

- FastAPI configured
- Project structure established
- Local execution working

---

Parent Epic: Epic: Project Management Workflow Provider

Define the YAML structure for project-management workflows, including provider configuration, epics, issues, labels, assignees, milestones, acceptance criteria, and provider metadata.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- PM workflow YAML schema is documented.
- Epics and issues are represented clearly.
- Optional fields such as labels, assignees, and milestones are supported.
- Provider-specific metadata can be stored without polluting the portable model.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- GitHub provider can authenticate using Jenkins credentials.
- Provider can create epic issues.
- Provider can create child issues.
- Provider can apply labels, assignees, and milestones when supplied.
- Provider errors are reported clearly.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Epic issue is created first.
- Child issues are created under the correct epic.
- Parent-child relationships are preserved.
- Created items are added to the configured project board.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Dry-run output lists epics and issues that would be created.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Existing epics are detected.
- Existing issues are detected.
- Matching can use provider ID when available.
- Matching can fall back to title when ID is missing.
- Duplicate creation is prevented.
- Dry-run output reports create/update/no-op actions.

- Provider URLs are written back to YAML.

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: Release Management

Document how StyxCD should separate infrastructure lifecycle from deployment lifecycle when Terraform or external infrastructure tools are involved.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Long-lived infrastructure responsibilities are identified.
- Release-time deployment responsibilities are identified.
- Fuzzy-boundary resources such as DNS, load balancers, target groups, ECS services, and ingress are discussed.
- Modes are documented: native deploy, Terraform-provisioned foundation, Terraform stage, and external infra contract.
- Decision supports future ECS and Terraform work.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: Multi-Cloud Support

Add EKS as a first-class StyxCD cloud workflow branch using the same conceptual deployment lifecycle proven by GKE, plus an EKS-specific load balancer controller preparation stage.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- CloudWorkflow emits EKS stages when platform.name is eks.
- EKS execution plan includes load balancer controller installation, namespace creation, per-app deploy/validate stages, ingress, DNS, and service validation.
- Jenkins stage map resolves all EKS stage classes.
- Hello-world EKS stage execution validates end-to-end stage ordering.
- Existing GKE workflow continues to work after EKS planner changes.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_
content.title: Implement EKS Execution Plan Shape

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: Multi-Cloud Support

Move the existing working EKS sandbox deployment logic into discrete StyxCD stages matching the new EKS execution plan.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- EksInstallLoadBalancerController installs or validates AWS Load Balancer Controller support.
- EksCreateNamespace ensures the application namespace exists.
- EksDeployApplication creates deployment, service, secrets, and environment configuration.
- EksValidateDeployment performs rollout validation and captures diagnostics.
- EksCreateIngress creates the ALB-backed Kubernetes ingress.
- EksConfigureDns updates Route53 records for the ALB ingress endpoint.
- EksValidateService validates public application reachability.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_
content.title: Implement EKS Stage Logic From Sandbox Code

items.title: Implement EKS Stage Logic From Sandbox Code

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: Multi-Cloud Support

Define the first formal EKS YAML model after the hello-world execution plan is validated.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- EKS YAML captures AWS region, cluster name, namespace, credentials, ingress class, ALB settings, DNS settings, and application mappings.
- The spec distinguishes portable Kubernetes concepts from EKS-specific implementation options.
- Example EKS YAML deploys Johnny-Johnny backend and UI.
- The EKS YAML can generate the validated EKS execution plan.
- Spec documentation explains differences from the GKE YAML.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_
content.title: Create Formal EKS YAML Specification

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: Multi-Cloud Support

Capture the architectural mapping between GKE and EKS stages, including where the workflows align conceptually and where EKS requires provider-specific setup.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Documentation includes a GKE/EKS stage mapping table.
- Documentation explains why EksInstallLoadBalancerController is separate from EksCreateIngress.
- Documentation explains the funnel model: cluster capability, namespace, app resources, ingress, DNS, validation.
- Documentation captures the principle that platform abstraction means common lifecycle plus provider-specific stage implementation.
- Document is stored in styxcd-docs.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_
content.title: Document GKE and EKS Stage Parity

items.title: Document GKE and EKS Stage Parity

Parent Epic: Epic: Multi-Cloud Support

Use the GKE and EKS stage model as the reference point for evaluating how AKS maps into the StyxCD execution lifecycle.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- AKS stage mapping is compared against GKE and EKS.
- Any AKS-specific platform preparation stages are identified.
- Planner impact is documented before implementation.
- AKS evaluation avoids premature over-abstraction.
- Decision is captured in architecture docs.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_
content.title: Evaluate AKS Stage Mapping

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: Multi-Cloud Support

Revisit ECS without Terraform first to discover the native ECS deployment lifecycle and compare it to Kubernetes platform workflows.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- ECS native deployment responsibilities are identified.
- Task definition, service update, load balancer, target group, DNS, and validation responsibilities are mapped.
- StyxCD ECS stages are proposed based on working implementation behavior.
- Terraform is deferred until native lifecycle boundaries are understood.
- ECS mapping is documented for later comparison.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_
content.title: Map ECS Native Deployment Lifecycle

Parent Epic: Epic: Artifact Management

Explore an enterprise artifact-management model where images are built once, tracked by digest, and promoted or replicated across cloud-native registries.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Canonical artifact metadata model is proposed.
- Digest-based deployment support is evaluated.
- Registry replication/promotion use cases are documented.
- Cost and complexity tradeoffs are captured.
- Feature is positioned as optional enterprise governance, not the default deployment path.

- Exceptions and execution-plan errors can flow to Loki/Grafana regardless of runtime.

Decide how StyxCD documentation should serve both human readers today and future retrieval-augmented generation use cases.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Human-readable documentation categories are defined.
- RAG-oriented documentation structure is defined.
- Document metadata conventions are proposed.
- Architecture decisions, YAML specs, examples, troubleshooting guides, and session handoffs are categorized.
- Guidelines explain what belongs in narrative docs versus machine-retrievable reference docs.

Create a formal documentation model for StyxCD YAML specs, including cloud workflow specs, PM workflow specs, examples, and help files.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- YAML fields are documented with purpose, required/optional status, examples, and provider-specific notes.
- GKE and EKS examples are included.
- Spec help files are designed to be readable by humans and useful for future assistant retrieval.
- Versioning strategy for YAML specs is documented.
- Effective YAML documentation requirements are captured.

Organize existing and future StyxCD docs into a clean structure that supports product documentation, architecture history, implementation notes, and AI retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Repository documentation folders are proposed.
- Architecture docs, ADRs, specs, examples, backlog YAML, and handoff docs have clear homes.
- Redundant or stale documents are identified.
- A naming convention for dated session/context docs is defined.
- Documentation cleanup plan is added to the roadmap.

- GKE/EKS stage parity decision is captured as an ADR.
- Native ECS before Terraform decision is captured as an ADR.
- Stable orchestration plus replaceable implementations principle is captured.
- ADRs are stored in styxcd-docs.

Revisit naming around VALIDATE_MAP, paramMap, and stageSpecificMap to better reflect execution planning, workflow context, and stage telemetry.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Candidate names are reviewed: PlanningDecision/DecisionMap, ExecutionContext, StageTelemetry.

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: Kharon Assistant

Create a retrieval layer that can index StyxCD documentation, specs, architecture decisions, backlog YAML, and session handoffs so Kharon can recover project context accurately.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Supported source document types are identified.
- Chunking and metadata strategy is defined.
- Retrieval results can include source references.
- Initial corpus includes architecture docs, YAML specs, backlog YAML, and handoff documents.
- Assistant can answer contextual questions about prior decisions without relying only on conversation memory.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_

Parent Epic: Epic: Kharon Assistant

Define which repositories, documents, and generated artifacts should become trusted sources for Kharon contextual answers.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Trusted source list is documented.
- styxcd-docs is treated as the primary project knowledge source.
- Generated session handoffs have a clear lifecycle.
- Deprecated or experimental documents are marked clearly.
- Source freshness and authority rules are documented.

_Source: styxcd-combined-backlog-2026-06-17(2).yml_
content.title: Define Kharon Project Memory Sources

content.body:
Parent Epic: Epic: StyxCD Command Line Tool

Create the initial StyxCD CLI command that provides parity with the current Next.js operations UI. The command should submit workflow YAML to the orchestrator and return execution metadata including execution ID, status, Jenkins links, Grafana links, and future execution details.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- CLI supports: styxcd ferry -f workflow.yml
- Workflow YAML can be submitted to the orchestrator.
- Execution ID is returned to the user.
- Execution status is returned to the user.
- Jenkins execution URL is returned when available.
- Grafana log URL is returned when available.
- Response can be displayed as structured JSON.
- Command behavior matches the current operations UI submission flow.

_Source: commandtool(1).yml_
content.title: Implement UI Parity Command Using Ferry Verb

items.title: Implement UI Parity Command Using Ferry Verb

content.body:
Create a portable project-management synchronization engine that treats

GitHub Projects as a planning interface while maintaining a

provider-independent Source of Truth (SSOT).

The synchronization engine must support bidirectional synchronization

between GitHub and the canonical backlog model while preserving

portability across future providers such as Jira, Bitbucket, Azure

DevOps, or Linear.
content.title: [Epic] Backlog-as-Code Synchronization

content.body:
-   Define portable backlog.yml schema.

-   Support epics, stories, acceptance criteria, provider metadata.
content.title: Design Canonical Backlog Model

content.body:
-   Read GitHub Projects, Issues, labels, milestones, priorities,

estimates, relationships.
content.title: Implement GitHub Project Export

content.body:
-   Stable deterministic output.

-   Idempotent generation.

-   Provider metadata isolated.
content.title: Generate Canonical `backlog.yml`

content.body:
-   Organized by epic.

-   Acceptance criteria included.

-   GitHub links included.
content.title: Generate Human-Readable `backlog.md`

content.body:
-   Detect additions, deletions, renames, status changes, priority

changes, moved stories.
content.title: Detect Backlog Changes

content.body:
-   Added, updated, removed, duplicate, warning, and error
